name: Actions Debug Workflow
description: Debug failing GitHub Actions workflows by viewing logs, re-running with debug logging, and testing locally.
trendingReason: GitHub Actions is the dominant CI/CD platform for open source

steps.title: List recent workflow runs
steps.description: View the status of recent workflow runs.

steps.title: View logs for a failed run
steps.description: Download and view the full logs from a specific workflow run.
steps.tip: Use --log-failed to see only the logs from failed steps, which is usually all you need.

steps.title: Re-run failed jobs with debug logging
steps.description: Re-run only the failed jobs with debug logging enabled for more verbose output.

steps.title: Watch a running workflow in real time
steps.description: Stream the output of a currently running workflow.
steps.tip: This is great for watching a run you just triggered without switching to the browser.

steps.title: Trigger a workflow manually
steps.description: Start a workflow_dispatch workflow from the command line.
steps.command: gh workflow run deploy.yml --ref main -f environment=staging

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Actions Debug Workflow
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Recent Workflow Runs ==="
gh run list --limit 10

echo ""
echo "=== Failed Runs ==="
gh run list --status failure --limit 5

echo ""
echo "=== Debug Commands ==="
echo "View logs:     gh run view <run-id> --log-failed"
echo "Full logs:     gh run view <run-id> --log"
echo "Re-run:        gh run rerun <run-id> --failed"
echo "Debug re-run:  gh run rerun <run-id> --failed --debug"
echo "Watch live:    gh run watch"
echo "Trigger:       gh workflow run <workflow.yml> --ref main"
echo "Cancel:        gh run cancel <run-id>"

faq.question: How do I enable debug logging for all runs?
faq.answer: Add a repository secret named ACTIONS_RUNNER_DEBUG with value 'true'. This enables verbose debug logging for all workflow runs.

faq.question: Can I test GitHub Actions locally?
faq.answer: Use the 'act' tool (https://github.com/nektos/act) to run GitHub Actions locally using Docker. It supports most action features.
